case_title: ROYAL INDEMNITY COMPANY VS. CHARTER OAK FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY et al

docket_entries.description: REPLY TO DEFT'S OPPOS. TO PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION FILED BY PETITIONER ROYAL INDEMNITY COMPANY

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER ROYAL INDEMNITY COMPANY'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED AS PREMATURE. ROYAL'S FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1330 IS TROUBLING, PARTICULARLY IN LIGHT OF THE REQUIREMENT THAT THE TVIAKI ACTION REMAINS UNRESOLVED WITH A PENDING TRIAL DATE. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Cheryl Orr, Esq. of Musick, Peeler & Garrett, 213-629-7881 for petitioner and moving party Royal Indemnity Co.
Linda Hsu, Esq. of Selman & Breitman, 415-979-0400 for respondent and opposing party Charter Oak Fire Insurance Co.

RULING:
ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING:
PETITIONER ROYAL INDEMNITY COMPANY'S Petition To Compel Arbitration IS DENIED AS PREMATURE. ROYAL'S FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1330 IS TROUBLING, PARTICULARLY IN LIGHT OF THE REQUIREMENT THAT THE TVIAKI ACTION REMAINS UNRESOLVED WITH A PENDING TRIAL DATE. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600 (302/PJM/VC)
